definitions: To ask someone to do something, especially to speak or recite in class.
To visit someone for a short period.
To formally request or appeal to someone for help or action.
To use a quality or resource that you have in order to achieve something.
examples: The teacher will call on students for answers.
We decided to call on our neighbors.
The organization will call on the community for support.
She had to call on all her strength to finish the race.
